name: ALMA Vortex Transitional Trend Detection
edge_hypothesis: ALMA (Arnaud Legoux MA) is designed for minimal lag with Gaussian smoothing — it detects trend shifts faster than EMA while being smoother than SMA. Vortex Indicator (VTXP/VTXM) independently measures trend direction using True Range geometry, completely uncorrelated with moving average calculations. Neither ALMA nor Vortex has EVER been tested. The thesis: when a fast-adaptive MA (ALMA) crosses a slow MA (SMA_50) AND an independent directional measure (Vortex) confirms, we catch genuine regime transitions early. ADX 15-30 gate targets the transitional regime where 4 strategies show PF 1.8-5.6 — an underexplored edge zone between ranging and trending.

variants.name: alma_vortex_1h_10to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: ALMA crossing SMA_50 fires exactly at trend transition moments — ALMA's Gaussian smoothing means this cross has less noise than EMA/EMA crosses. Vortex confirmation eliminates false crosses (VTXP > VTXM = genuine upward pressure). ADX 15-30 band targets transitional regime — enough direction to sustain a move but early enough to capture the full swing. 10:1 R:R for the expected transitional-to-trending continuation.

variants.name: alma_vortex_4h_12to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 4h ALMA/SMA cross is a rare, high-conviction regime transition signal. At 4h, the ADX 15-30 transitional window represents multi-day regime shifts. 12:1 R:R captures the full expected trend development from transitional into trending. Wider 2.0x stop for 4h volatility. If transitional regime is truly an edge zone (4 strategies show PF 1.8-5.6), this should outperform pure trend-following by entering earlier.
